// <auto-generated>This file has been auto generated from 'Qyl.OpenTelemetry.SemanticConventions.SourceGeneration'.</auto-generated>

#nullable enable

#pragma warning disable CS1570 // XML comment has badly formed XML

namespace OpenTelemetry.SemanticConventions;

/// <summary>
/// Typed factory extensions for OpenTelemetry semantic-convention metric instruments.
/// Each method creates an instrument with the registry-defined name, unit, and description.
/// </summary>
static partial class HttpServerIncubatingMeters
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Number of active HTTP server requests.
    /// </summary>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Metrics.UpDownCounter<long> CreateHttpServerActiveRequestsUpdowncounter(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Metrics.Meter meter)
        => meter.CreateUpDownCounter<long>(
            name: "http.server.active_requests",
            unit: "{request}",
            description: "Number of active HTTP server requests.");

    /// <summary>
    /// Size of HTTP server request bodies.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// The size of the request payload body in bytes. This is the number of bytes transferred excluding headers and is often, but not always, present as the [Content-Length](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#field.content-length) header. For requests using transport encoding, this should be the compressed size.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Metrics.Histogram<long> CreateHttpServerRequestBodySizeHistogram(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Metrics.Meter meter)
        => meter.CreateHistogram<long>(
            name: "http.server.request.body.size",
            unit: "By",
            description: "Size of HTTP server request bodies.");

    /// <summary>
    /// Duration of HTTP server requests.
    /// </summary>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Metrics.Histogram<double> CreateHttpServerRequestDurationHistogram(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Metrics.Meter meter)
        => meter.CreateHistogram<double>(
            name: "http.server.request.duration",
            unit: "s",
            description: "Duration of HTTP server requests.");

    /// <summary>
    /// Size of HTTP server response bodies.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// The size of the response payload body in bytes. This is the number of bytes transferred excluding headers and is often, but not always, present as the [Content-Length](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#field.content-length) header. For requests using transport encoding, this should be the compressed size.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Metrics.Histogram<long> CreateHttpServerResponseBodySizeHistogram(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Metrics.Meter meter)
        => meter.CreateHistogram<long>(
            name: "http.server.response.body.size",
            unit: "By",
            description: "Size of HTTP server response bodies.");
}
